learning_outcomes.description: Understand the critical strategic importance of innovation for all organisations.
Critically evaluate the nature and context of the innovation process in the sustainable energy sector and how it is changing.
Demonstrate competence in adapting and applying the strategic and operational managerial frameworks and tools necessary for innovation leadership.
Interpret and apply the theory of disruptive innovation to market and industry trends.
Using teamwork skills, develop an innovation strategy for an organisation, illustrating insight in the application of innovation themes and theories.

assessments.task_description:
Following the material covered in the intensive classes, your task is to create a photo essay and 500-word written reflection activity.


Refer to the course Blackboard site for further details.

This task has been designed to evaluate your understanding of the concepts covered in this course and to assess your abilities in applying these concepts. You are therefore required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of your written submission independent of AI and MT tools. If you choose to use generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) technologies to support your learning, you must include an AI acknowledgement coversheet template and ensure that you are complying with UQ guidelines.

A failure to reference your use of generative AI or MT use may therefore constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Innovation Strategy Project Pitch and Report
assessments.due_date:
Teams will complete their project pitch during the first workshop, on either 11th or 13th August (depending on the workshop for which you are registered). However, all teams are required to submit their presentation slides via the link provided in Blackboard by 11th August at 4pm. 11/08/2026 4:00 pm

Final project reports are due on Monday 14th September at 9am. 14/09/2026 9:00 am
assessments.task_description:
Task Description:

This is a group assignment that will be completed in teams (4-5 people). Your overall grade (out of 45%) will be calculated from three parts: (1) project pitch (out of 5%), (2) overall team report score (out of 20%), and (3) individual score for your own independent section of the report (out of 20%).

The overarching goal of this task is to build evidence for a new energy-related innovation, testing and iterating as you progress your ideas throughout the semester. Your team will choose an innovation (e.g. new idea, product, service) on which to focus. Together, your team will also decide on the context for your business challenge, e.g. a) an independent start-up, b) organization you aim work for in future, or c) your current or past employer.

Your teams will be formed during the intensive classes in Week 2. During those classes, you will also assign roles and responsibilities with mentorship from the teaching team.

Project Pitch (5% of the course grade):

During your first workshop (on either 11th or 13th August), your team will make a project pitch (maximum 10 minutes, plus 5 minutes questions). This will provide an opportunity for your team to receive feedback on your chosen innovation and context before you progress in preparing your written report. All teams are required to submit their presentation slides by the same date and time.

Report (worth 40% in total; with 20% of your grade from the team report score and 20% from your individual section of the report)

Your team will submit one collated report by the report due date. Some of these sections will contribute to your 'team report score' while others will contribute to your 'individual score'.

Further information regarding the requirements for the pitch and the report, and the associated grading rubrics, are available on the course Blackboard site. Your presentation slides and reports are to be submitted on Blackboard via the links provided.

This task has been designed to evaluate your understanding of the concepts covered in this course and to assess your abilities in applying these concepts. You are therefore required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of your written submission independent of AI and MT tools. If you choose to use generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) technologies to support your learning, you must include an AI acknowledgement coversheet template and ensure that you are complying with UQ guidelines.

A failure to reference your use of generative AI or MT use may therefore constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All assessment items should be submitted through the links provided on the ENGY7200 Blackboard site. TurnItIn will be used for the reports.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Memo for all students is due at 9am on 14th September 14/09/2026 9:00 am

Individual interviews (hurdle) with each student will be held over 17 and 18 September 17/09/2026
assessments.task_description:
Students will be provided with a scenario by the instructor in the form of a Podcast. The assessment for this item comprises of two parts:

(1) Memo (800 words) responding to a project brief provided by the instructor (worth 10%), and

(2) Individual oral interview (HURDLE: worth 20%).

For the Memo, you may choose to use generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) technologies to support your learning. If so, you must include an AI acknowledgement coversheet template and ensure that you are complying with UQ guidelines.

The Individual Oral Interview evaluates students' abilities, skills, and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI). Students are advised that the use of AI technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All assessment items should be submitted through the links provided on the ENGY7200 Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
